RHIDP-12205: CQA 2.1 compliance for Audit logs in Red Hat Developer Hub (#1877)

* RHIDP-12205: CQA 2.1 compliance for Audit logs in Red Hat Developer Hub title

Apply CQA 2.1 standards for DITA 1.3 compatibility and AEM migration readiness:
- Add short descriptions with [role="_abstract"] to assembly and all modules
- Remove DITA-incompatible block title delimiters (--) from example blocks
- Convert callout list to description list format
- Apply one sentence per line formatting to substep descriptions
- Fix Red Hat style: change "using" to "by using"

Validation results: Vale DITA (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 suggestions) and Vale Red Hat style (0 errors, 0 warnings)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Fabrice Flore-Thébault <ffloreth@redhat.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

* RHIDP-12205: Apply code review suggestions and align IDs with titles

Apply all inline code review suggestions from lhite8041 and update module IDs
to follow CQA guidelines (no proc- prefix, match title exactly).

Changes to proc-audit-log-view.adoc:
- Updated ID: proc-audit-log-view → view-audit-logs-in-product-short
- Improved abstract: Split into two sentences for clarity
- Fixed Red Hat style: "using" → "by using" after noun

Changes to proc-forward-audit-log-splunk.adoc:
- Updated title: "Forwarding" → "Forward" (gerund to imperative)
- Updated ID: proc-forward-audit-log-splunk → forward-product-audit-logs-to-splunk
- Combined abstract: Merged abstract and following sentence into single, complete sentence
- Improved AtLeastOnce description: More concise wording per review suggestion

All changes maintain Vale DITA compliance (0 errors, 0 warnings) and address
reviewer feedback for improved clarity and consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* RHIDP-12205: Convert audit log configuration from concept to procedure module

Convert con-audit-log-config.adoc from a concept module to a procedure module
following modular documentation templates.

Changes:
- Changed content type: CONCEPT → PROCEDURE
- Updated title: "Configuring audit logs" → "Configure audit logs" (gerund to imperative)
- Updated file name: con-audit-log-config.adoc → proc-configure-audit-logs.adoc
- Updated ID to follow CQA guidelines:
  - Old: con-audit-log-config_{context}
  - New: configure-audit-logs-for-product-short-on-ocp-short_{context}
- Restructured content to follow procedure template:
  - Added Prerequisites section
  - Converted description lists to numbered procedure steps with substeps
  - Moved Additional resources links into procedure steps for context
- Updated include statement in assembly-audit-log.adoc

The original content described configuration steps, which is procedural content,
so this conversion aligns the module type with its actual content.

Vale DITA validation: 0 errors, 0 warnings
Vale Red Hat style: 0 errors (4 URL warnings are false positives)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Fabrice Flore-Thébault <ffloreth@redhat.com>

* RHIDP-12205: Align procedure module file names with titles in audit log

Renamed procedure modules to match their titles following CQA 2.1 naming conventions:
- proc-audit-log-view.adoc → proc-view-audit-logs-in-product-short.adoc
- proc-forward-audit-log-splunk.adoc → proc-forward-product-audit-logs-to-splunk.adoc

Updated include statements in assembly-audit-log.adoc to reference renamed files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* RHIDP-12205: Simplify abstract and use terminal source type for commands

Simplified the abstract in proc-forward-product-audit-logs-to-splunk.adoc from 217 to 113 characters by removing implementation details.

Changed all terminal commands from [source,bash] to [source,terminal] to follow the correct convention for terminal commands versus bash scripts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* RHIDP-12205: Remove passive voice from procedure

Converted passive voice to active voice in proc-forward-product-audit-logs-to-splunk.adoc to improve clarity and follow Red Hat style guidelines:
- "where the captured logs are sent" → "where to send the captured logs"
- "Tuning can be applied" → "You can apply tuning"
- "are re-sent" → "the forwarder re-sends"
- "logs are duplicated" → "forwarder might duplicate some logs"
- "logs are being forwarded" → "Splunk instance receives logs"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* RHIDP-12205: Update CQA.md with procedure writing guidelines

Added new sections to CQA.md based on learnings from audit log work:
- Source code block types: Use [source,terminal] for terminal commands, not [source,bash]
- Voice and tense in procedures: Avoid passive voice, use present tense
- Abstract guidelines: Keep concise (50-300 chars), focus on essential action

These guidelines help ensure procedures follow Red Hat style and DITA compliance requirements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Fabrice Flore-Thébault <ffloreth@redhat.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix file watcher leak on repo switch and save/reload race condition

Stop watcher before refresh so switching repos replaces the old
polling task. Add savesInFlight guard to prevent the watcher from
overwriting optimistic UI updates with stale disk data. Add deinit
cleanup for the watcher task.

commit.message:
Fix TMI PER en sortie capital + frais souscription SCPI sur réinvestissement

1. PER sortie capital : la TMI retraite incluait une rente PER fictive même
   en mode croquerCapital, gonflant la TMI (ex: 30% au lieu de 11%).
   Fix : rentePerBruteEstimee = 0 quand croquerCapital = true.

2. SCPI : les distributions réinvesties composaient à 4.5% sans friction.
   En réalité, réinvestir en SCPI coûte ~10% de frais de souscription.
   Fix : rendementScpiEffectif = rendementScpi × (1 − partDistrib × frais),
   soit ~4.1% au lieu de 4.5% (~0.4 pt/an de surestimation corrigée).

3. Documenté la limitation connue du drawdown par annuité fixe vs simulation
   avec rendements distincts par enveloppe (nécessiterait une bisection).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 streaming thread safety and persistent connection isolation (#5229)

* fix: suppress HttpServerH3Streaming debug output when verbose < 3

Replace @debug printf() calls in StreamingBodyHandler with verbose > 2
checks, passing the test's verbose level to the handler constructor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 streaming thread safety and persistent connection isolation

- Fix RST_STREAM race: remove resetHttp2Stream/cleanupHttp2Stream from the
  persistent handler after queuing responses; remove RST_STREAM from
  onDataChunkRecvCallback for cleaned-up server streams. Prevents queued
  error responses from being cancelled before nghttp2 can flush them.

- Fix HTTP/3 persistent queue isolation: include session_id in the
  persistent queue key (hash:quic:session_id) so each QUIC session gets
  its own persistent queue instead of sharing the UDP listener's key.

- Fix per-thread persistence leak: staticPersistenceCleanup() now removes
  _persistent_threads map entries via the handler reference stored in
  thread-local data, preventing stale persistence on reused pool threads.

- Fix multiplexed send abort: replace session-global hasCompletedStreams()
  with stream-specific isStreamComplete(stream_id) in the HTTP/2 client
  streaming body send path, preventing unrelated stream completions from
  aborting the current upload on multiplexed connections.

- Add op_lock mutex to Http2PollOperation for thread safety between I/O
  thread (continuePoll) and handler threads (startSendResponse, etc.)

- Add Queue-based async data delivery for HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 extended
  CONNECT body streaming with CV-based flow control backpressure

- Add getPushResourceCallback() with lock protection for HTTP/2 server push

- Add retry limit (200) to HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 send_stream_data flow control

- Add resume_reading on_exit block for exception safety in persistent handler

- Add persistent connection support in HttpServer multiplexed sync handler,
  RestHandler body streaming error propagation, and HttpServerUtil
  persistent queue filter mechanism

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve CI build errors and HTTP/2 end-stream backpressure hang

- Add missing #include <condition_variable> in Http2Session.h
- Fix QoreValue::c_str() calls in QoreHttpClientObject.cpp (use
  get<const QoreStringNode>()->c_str() instead)
- Allow zero-length end_stream sends to bypass buffer backpressure check
  in Http2Session::sendStreamData(); previously, sending end_stream with
  no data when the buffer was full raised HTTP2-FLOW-CONTROL, causing the
  handler-thread streaming fallback to hang indefinitely
- Clean up leftover debug output in QoreSocketObject.cpp and QoreSocket.cpp

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address copilot review — HTTP/2 stream timeout and QUIC resetStream cleanup

- readHttp2StreamDataBlock() now raises HTTP2-STREAM-TIMEOUT on timeout
  instead of returning NOTHING silently, matching readQuicStreamDataBlock()
  behavior and preventing callers from confusing timeout with END_STREAM
- QuicSession::resetStream() now erases connect_stream_data_ under
  connect_data_mutex_ to avoid leaking buffered extended-CONNECT tunnel
  data for reset streams

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* reenabled templates and values for where_cond in the pipeline filter action

* fix: closure class context, TAG_ENUM container preservation, HTTP/2 drain race, and ProviderIndexUtil cleanup

- QoreClosureParseNode::evalClosure() now captures runtime class context so
  closures created in static methods retain access to private methods when
  executed from a different class context
- QoreTypeSpec::acceptInput() QTS_TYPE case: only unwrap TAG_ENUM for direct
  lvalue/parameter assignments, not for hash key or member assignments, so
  enum values survive round-trips through hash<auto> containers
- Http2Session::notifyStreamDrain(): move drain_gen_ increment inside
  drain_mtx_ lock to prevent lost-wakeup race with waitForStreamDrain()
- ProviderIndexUtil.qc: remove unnecessary try/catch around UiOptionViewMap
  constant lookup since DataProvider is released with Qore in the same repo

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ingest): retry reset when desktop still linked after startup

Initial reset call fails silently when Signal Desktop container isn't
reachable yet. Desktop then comes up still linked from previous session,
and the QR poll loop waits 120s for an unlink that never happens.

refactor(db): squash 57 migrations into single Init migration

Replace 57 incremental migrations with one squashed Init migration
generated from entity synchronization against a fresh PostgreSQL.

This fixes the CI E2E test failures caused by enum/index duplication
when migrations ran sequentially on a clean database.

Changes:
- Delete 57 old migration files
- Add 1700000000000-Init.ts (full schema: 280 types, 221 tables,
  744 indexes, 196 FKs)
- Fix 7 duplicate @Index() decorators across 4 entity files
  (class-level + property-level on same column)
- Railway typeorm_migrations table updated to reference new Init

fix(ingest): patient sync for large accounts (10K+ conversations)

Signal Desktop sync IS working for large accounts — it just takes
10-20 minutes. Previous code gave up after 2 min or on false stall
detection. Now:
- Poll every 10s (less pressure on Signal Desktop SQLite)
- Only consider sync stalled after 5 min of zero new conversations
- Log progress with conversation/group counts
- Removed broken retry/re-QR logic (sync works, just needs patience)
- 1 hour absolute timeout

Tested: account with 10,944 conversations synced 63 groups in ~8 min.

commit.message:
setup.sh: fix wineboot by running headless; use unset to enforce it

Wine 11 wineboot fails with DISPLAY set:
  start_rpcss Failed to open RpcSs service
  run_wineboot boot event wait timed out
  wine: could not load kernel32.dll, status c0000135

Wineboot initialises the Wine prefix fine in headless mode. The root
cause was commit 82d841c moving DISPLAY=:0 to before wineboot as part
of a cleanup, not realising the original placement (after wineboot) was
intentional.

Use `unset DISPLAY` rather than just not setting it, so the constraint
holds even if DISPLAY is present in the caller's environment. DISPLAY is
then set immediately before winetricks, which needs X for font installs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 10 bugs found in code audit

- form_finder: search filter "s"/"q" matched any field containing the letter, skipping legitimate fields like address/message

- gemini: dir() checked module scope instead of locals() for error variable
- database: connection leak on exceptions - added try/finally to update_site and add_submission
- main: asyncio.shield on already-cancelled task replaced with direct await

- settings.html: f.value.toLowerCase() crashed on null toggle values

Add transparent auto-login for ZUEB authenticated commands

When a session is missing or a token expires mid-flight, the system
now automatically re-authenticates using ZUEB_USERNAME / ZUEB_PASSWORD
from the environment, making nightly scheduler and other unattended
flows resilient to token expiry without user intervention.

- Add _auto_login / _ensure_session / _is_sso_error / _run_with_relogin
  helpers to bot/agent/helper.py
- Refactor schedule, attendance, attendance-punch to use the new helpers
  (L2: auto-login on missing session; L3: retry once on SSO failure)
- Make login command args optional with env-var fallback
- /login handler in commands.py supports no-arg env-var login;
  message deletion skipped when no password was in the message
- Update system prompt rule 4: agent goes directly to skills instead
  of prompting for /login first
